children.label: A
children.text: to perform and complete a survey of the Area; or

children.label: B
children.text: to carry out any other project or activity for the benefit of the Area in accordance with sections 539m to 539m–12 of this title.

children.heading: Deadline
children.label: 2 Deadline
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Secretary shall complete the survey of the Area under paragraph (1)(A).February 20, 2003

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , after consultation with the Pueblo, the Secretary shall, in accordance with applicable laws, prepare and offer a land exchange of National Forest land outside the Area and contiguous to the northern boundary of the Pueblo’s Reservation within sections 3, 10, 11, and 14 of T12N, R4E, N.M.P.M., Sandoval County, New Mexico excluding wilderness land, for land owned by the Pueblo in the Evergreen Hills subdivision in Sandoval County contiguous to National Forest land, and the La Luz tract in Bernalillo County.February 20, 2003

children.heading: Acceptance of payment
children.label: 2 Acceptance of payment
children.text: Notwithstanding , the Secretary may either make or accept a cash equalization payment in excess of 25 percent of the total value of the land or interests transferred out of Federal ownership.section 1716(b) of title 43

children.heading: Funds received
children.label: 3 Funds received
children.text: Any funds received by the Secretary as a result of the exchange shall be deposited in the fund established under , and shall be available to purchase non-Federal land within or adjacent to the National Forests in the State of New Mexico.section 484a of this title

children.heading: Treatment of land exchanged or conveyed
children.label: 4 Treatment of land exchanged or conveyed
children.text: All land exchanged or conveyed to the Pueblo is declared to be held in trust for the Pueblo by the United States and added to the Pueblo’s Reservation subject to all existing and outstanding rights and shall, as a condition of the title to be conveyed, remain in its natural state and shall not be subject to commercial development of any kind. Land exchanged or conveyed to the Forest Service shall be subject to all limitations on use pertaining to the Area under sections 539m to 539m–12 of this title.

children.heading: Failure to make offer
children.label: 5 Failure to make offer
children.text: If the land exchange offer is not made by the date that is 180 days after , the Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources of the United States Senate and the Committee on Resources of the United States House of Representatives, a report explaining the reasons for the failure to make the offer including an assessment of the need for any additional legislation that may be necessary for the exchange. If additional legislation is not necessary, the Secretary, consistent with this section, should proceed with the exchange pursuant to existing law.February 20, 2003

children.label: i
children.text: subject to the restriction enforced by the Secretary of the Interior that the land remain undeveloped, with the natural characteristics of the land to be preserved in perpetuity; and

children.label: ii
children.text: consistent with subsection (c).

children.label: I
children.text: the La Luz tract generally depicted on the map entitled “Sandia Pueblo Settlement Technical Amendment Act” and dated ; andOctober 18, 2013

children.label: II
children.text: the conservation easement for the Piedra Lisa tract generally depicted on the map entitled “Sandia Pueblo Settlement Technical Amendment Act” and dated ; andOctober 18, 2013

children.label: ii
children.text: grant to the Secretary a right-of-way for the Piedra Lisa Trail within the Piedra Lisa tract generally depicted on the map entitled “Sandia Pueblo Settlement Technical Amendment Act” and dated .October 18, 2013

children.heading: Land acquisition and other compensation
children.label: c Land acquisition and other compensation

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may acquire land owned by the Pueblo within the Evergreen Hills Subdivision in Sandoval County or any other privately held land inside of the exterior boundaries of the Area. The boundaries of the Cibola National Forest and the Area shall be adjusted to encompass any land acquired pursuant to this section.

children.label: A
children.text: the right-of-way established pursuant to ; andsection 539m–7(h)(3)(C) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the conservation easement established by the limitations on use of the Piedra Lisa tract pursuant to .section 539m–7(b)(2) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: dues or payments to a homeowner association for the purpose of legal representation; and

children.label: B
children.text: legal fees and related expenses.

children.heading: Treatment of reimbursement
children.label: 2 Treatment of reimbursement
children.text: Any reimbursement provided in this subsection shall be in lieu of that which might otherwise be available pursuant to the Equal Access to Justice Act (24 U.S.C. 2412).1So in original. Probably should be “28”.1

children.heading: Payments
children.label: 3 Payments
children.text: Subject to the availability of appropriated funds the Secretary of the Treasury shall make reimbursement payments as provided in this section.

children.heading: Applications
children.label: 4 Applications
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , applications for reimbursement shall be filed with the Department of the Treasury, Financial Management Service, Washington, D.C.February 20, 2003

children.heading: Maximum reimbursement
children.label: 5 Maximum reimbursement
children.text: No party shall be reimbursed in excess of $750,000 under this section, and the total amount reimbursed in accordance with this section shall not exceed $3,000,000.
